problem.title: Jump Game
problem.description: You are given an integer array nums. You are initially positioned at the array's first index, and each element in the array represents your maximum jump length at that position.
assumptions: nums[i] = max jump length from index i.
Return true if you can reach the last index.

example.explanation: Jump 1 step from index 0 to 1, then 3 steps to last index. Always track max reachable index.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Greedy [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Track the farthest index reachable from any position visited so far; if the current index ever exceeds that maximum reach, the end cannot be reached and the function returns false. Otherwise, update the maximum reach at each step and return true at the end. The key insight is that we never need to enumerate specific jump paths — simply knowing the furthest reachable index is sufficient to determine feasibility.

solutions.title: Solution 2 – Dynamic Programming [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Define dp[i] = true if index i is reachable from index 0. For each reachable index i, mark all indices i+1 through i+nums[i] as reachable. Return dp[n-1]. O(n²) worst case but illustrates the DP framing interviewers often ask for before accepting the greedy.
